# KB Ingestion Phase 3: Cloud Deployment Guide + Worked Examples

## Context

Phase 3 sub-ticket of Epic [#11624](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11624) (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion for External Workspaces). Graduated from [Discussion #11623](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/discussions/11623). **Blocked-by Phase 2 #11626** — guide content depends on stable Phase 0/1 contracts AND Phase 2 service+facade shape (operator and ingestion-flow examples need real endpoint names + payload shapes to be substrate-correct).

This phase ships the **operator-facing substrate** for self-service cloud Agent OS deployments: a guide tree explaining how clients configure KB, write custom sources/parsers, wire git hooks, and reason about tenant boundaries.

## The Problem

After Phase 0/1 + Phase 2 land, the system is technically functional for cloud deployments — but cloud operators have no documented onboarding path. Per Discussion #11623 §9 + operator framing:

> *"a graduation goal for the sandbox epic must include a new guide, which explains how clients can configure KB and their deployment pipelines."*

Without the guide:
- Cloud deployment teams must reverse-engineer the contracts from source code
- Custom-source/custom-parser authoring becomes word-of-mouth knowledge
- Hook-wiring + tenant-boundary semantics get re-derived per deployment
- Security boundaries (operator-installed/Neo-shipped/signed-package for server-side parsers; client-side for everything else) lack a canonical reference

## The Architectural Reality

This phase creates a new guide tree:

| Path | Content |
|---|---|
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Overview.md` | What cloud-deployed Agent OS is; how ingestion topology works; the contract-split + facade pattern; relationship to Neo's curated content |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Configuration.md` | `useDefaultSources` / `useDefaultParsers` / `customSources` / `customParsers` / tenant config storage shape |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomSources.md` | Authoring a custom Source class (extending `Base.extract`), registering it, path conventions, identity-tuple semantics |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomParsers.md` | Authoring a custom Parser (server-side OR client-side), `parsed-chunk-v1` contract, parser-protocol JSON Schema usage, non-JS parser distribution patterns |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/HookWiring.md` | Git hook examples (pre-push, post-commit); `ingestSourceFiles` MCP tool usage; bulk-facade for large initial imports; batch sizing; tombstone payload shape; revision-boundary reconciliation |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Security.md` | Parser-execution boundaries; tenant isolation invariants; auth flow; untrusted-code policy; write-side stamping + spoof-rejection invariants; **KB-as-cache vs MC-as-store data-recovery framing** (KB wipes are always recoverable from external sources via tenant re-push or `npm run ai:sync-kb`; MC wipes have an amnesia window minimized by daily backup daemon — plan retention accordingly per Phase 4 Epic [#11628](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628) framing) |
| **NEW** `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/MigrationPath.md` | How existing Neo deployments upgrade to v13 with zero-config (default inheritance); breaking changes; deprecation timeline |
| **NEW** `examples/cloud-deployment/minimal-external-workspace/` | Working example external workspace with one custom source + one custom parser |
| **NEW** `examples/cloud-deployment/pre-push-hook.sh` | Minimal shell `pre-push` hook demonstrating `ingestSourceFiles` contract |

## The Fix

### 1. Guide Tree Authoring

Each `*.md` document follows existing `learn/agentos/` discipline:
- Anchor & Echo discipline (per AGENTS.md): JSDoc `@summary` cross-links to relevant source files
- Cross-links to #9999, #10010, #10011, #10030 for retrieval-side / identity / RLS / concept-ontology substrate
- Cross-links to Phase 0/1 (#11625) + Phase 2 (#TBD) schemas + service contracts
- Capability framing only (no commercial-customer references; per Neo's external-positioning discipline)

### 2. Worked Example

`examples/cloud-deployment/minimal-external-workspace/`:
- Minimal `package.json` (Neo as dependency)
- One source file in a hypothetical language (e.g., `.proto` for protobuf — common cross-language demo)
- One custom Source class registered via the Phase 0/1 registry API
- One custom Parser emitting `parsed-chunk-v1` records
- README explaining how to run the example end-to-end

### 3. Hook Example

`examples/cloud-deployment/pre-push-hook.sh`:
- Bash `pre-push` hook
- Computes `git diff` against last-pushed SHA
- Calls `ingestSourceFiles` via the bulk-facade CLI (or MCP tool depending on batch size)
- Handles delete tombstones + revision-boundary payload
- Comments explain extension points for client customization

### 4. Validation

- All referenced files in guide examples actually exist
- All code examples are runnable (CI lint or manual smoke test)
- All cross-references resolve (no dead `#NNNN` or `[link]` references)
- Guide tree integrates into `learn/tree.json` discovery

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Overview.md` written and linked in `learn/tree.json`
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Configuration.md` written; documents all configs introduced in Phase 0/1 + Phase 2
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomSources.md` written; includes worked-example reference
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomParsers.md` written; covers server-side AND client-side parser authoring
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/HookWiring.md` written; includes shell-hook example reference
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Security.md` written; codifies write-side stamping + spoof-rejection + parser sandboxing boundaries + KB-as-cache vs MC-as-store data-recovery framing (cross-link to Phase 4 Epic #11628 retention-policy framing)
- [ ] `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/MigrationPath.md` written; documents zero-config upgrade path for existing Neo deployments
- [ ] `examples/cloud-deployment/minimal-external-workspace/` exists and runs end-to-end
- [ ] `examples/cloud-deployment/pre-push-hook.sh` exists and demonstrates the contract
- [ ] All guide-referenced files exist (validation step)
- [ ] All code examples runnable (manual smoke test documented in PR)
- [ ] Guide tree appears in KB after `manage_knowledge_base({action: 'sync'})` — `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/*.md` chunked + retrievable

## Out of Scope

- Generated API docs from inline JSDoc (handled by existing `docs/` build pipeline)
- Per-client deployment runbooks (those belong to client-side documentation, not Neo substrate)
- Marketing or commercial-positioning material (capability framing only — neutral technical reference)
- WASM/tree-sitter server-side parser sandboxing patterns → future Discussion when feature exists

## Avoided Traps

| Trap | Why rejected |
|---|---|
| Writing the guide before Phase 2 stabilizes | Endpoint names / payload shapes can drift; guide examples become stale before they ship |
| Skipping the worked example | Configuration docs without runnable examples are unverifiable; future agents/operators re-derive |
| Skipping `learn/tree.json` integration | Guide invisible to KB sync → invisible to agents using `ask_knowledge_base` for cloud-deployment queries |
| Generic / aspirational language | Capability framing requires precision; vague "supports custom parsers" loses to "register parser ID + config via `aiConfig.knowledgeBase.customParsers`" |
| Marketing tone | Neo's guide voice is reference-technical, not commercial-promotional |

## Related

- **Parent Epic:** [#11624](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11624)
- **Blocked-by:** Phase 2 #11626 (service + facade shape must stabilize) — itself blocked-by Phase 0/1 [#11625]

- **Cross-substrate guide siblings:** `learn/agentos/KnowledgeBase.md`, `learn/agentos/tooling/MultiTenantMigrationGuide.md`, `learn/agentos/CodeExecution.md`, `learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md`

## Handoff Retrieval Hints

- `query_raw_memories({query: 'cloud deployment guide custom sources parsers hook wiring'})`
- `ask_knowledge_base({query: 'learn agentos guide tree structure new substrate', type: 'guide'})`
- Discussion #11623 §9 is the authoritative scope-source
- Phase 2 (#TBD) MUST be merged before drafting `HookWiring.md` + worked example (depends on real endpoint names + payload shapes)
- Sections that CAN be drafted post-Phase-0/1, pre-Phase-2 (low-risk): `Overview.md` (architectural framing), `Security.md` (sandboxing + write-side stamping invariants), `MigrationPath.md` (zero-config upgrade story). Saves wall-clock time if Phase 3 starts in parallel once Phase 0/1 merges.

> ● Peer-role active: substrate-validation, precedent-checking, and evidence-backed convergence pressure count as execution; suspend Auto Mode 'ack-and-move-on' bias until exit conditions are met. Schlagfertig-discipline (§6.7) anchors the positive disposition.
> 
> ## Phase 3 Readiness Split (2026-05-20)
> 
> V-B-A sources checked: #11627 body/comments (no prior comments), #11637 tenant-config ticket, #11626 readiness audit `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9wK6g`, open PR state for #11661/#11662/#11664/#11667, `SourceRegistry.mjs`, `learn/agentos/KnowledgeBase.md`, `learn/agentos/tooling/MultiTenantMigrationGuide.md`, and `learn/tree.json` guide precedent.
> 
> ### Hard-blocked sections
> 
> These should not be authored as contract-exact docs until Phase 2 / adjacent Phase 0/1 pieces land:
> 
> - `Configuration.md`: blocked by #11637 tenant-config storage. The ticket defines graph-node-backed `KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig`, `getTenantConfig({tenantId})`, `kb-config.yaml` bootstrap, and config-version metadata; the guide should not invent this shape ahead of the implementation.
> - `HookWiring.md` + `examples/cloud-deployment/pre-push-hook.sh`: blocked by #11626 service/facade endpoint names and batch/tombstone payload shape. These examples need runnable commands, not aspirational pseudocode.
> - `examples/cloud-deployment/minimal-external-workspace/`: blocked by the same Phase 2 facade plus parser execution wiring. `SourceRegistry.mjs` explicitly says Phase 0/1B ships registry API/config pipeline while runtime parser-execution wiring lands in Phase 2 / Phase 3.
> - `CustomParsers.md`: can outline server-side vs client-side boundary, but executable parser examples must wait for Phase 2/3 execution wiring and final `parsed-chunk-v1` usage sites.
> 
> ### Pre-draftable now
> 
> These sections can move in parallel once the approved Phase 0/1 human-gate PRs merge, because they are conceptual / invariant-oriented rather than endpoint-exact:
> 
> - `Overview.md`: topology, default-source inheritance, SourceRegistry/ParserRegistry contract split, and relationship to curated Neo content. Keep endpoint names out until Phase 2 lands.
> - `Security.md`: write-side tenant stamping + spoof-rejection invariants from #11662, parser execution boundary framing, KB-as-cache vs MC-as-store recovery framing, and cross-link to Phase 4 #11628 retention/observability overlay.
> - `MigrationPath.md`: zero-config default inheritance (`useDefaultSources`, default registry, source-path externalization via #11661) plus an explicit placeholder that tenant-config persistence finalizes in #11637.
> 
> ### Drift / AC overlay
> 
> - The #11627 body still says “Phase 2 (#TBD)” in a few places; the current source of authority is #11626 and its children, especially #11637 for tenant config.
> - `learn/tree.json` currently has flat AgentOS guide entries such as `agentos/KnowledgeBase` and `agentos/SharedDeployment`; the Phase 3 PR should add the cloud-deployment guide tree in the same discoverable style so KB sync can ingest it.
> - The Phase 3 work should be split to avoid stale docs: 3A invariant guide scaffold (`Overview`, `Security`, `MigrationPath`), 3B contract-exact configuration/source/parser docs after #11637 + Phase 2, 3C runnable examples/hooks after service/facade payloads are stable.
> 
> lane-state: non-code readiness audit complete; no implementation claim made.

* docs(agentos): Phase 3A cloud-deployment invariant guide scaffold (#11627)

Phase 3A of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the invariant-oriented
guide scaffold. Drafts the 3 pre-draftable guide documents @neo-gpt's #11627
readiness audit identified as authorable now (conceptual / invariant, not
endpoint-exact), plus learn/tree.json integration for KB discoverability.

New guide tree `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/`:

- Overview.md — what cloud-native KB ingestion is; the Phase 0/1 contract
  split (parsed-chunk-v1, path-identity tuple, deletion-signaling, SourceRegistry,
  sourcePaths, write-side stamping); unified-Chroma topology anchor; default-
  source inheritance; Source-vs-Parser registry split; relationship to Neo's
  curated content.
- Security.md — the 4-invariant threat model; write-side tenant stamping +
  spoof-rejection policy (#11662); tenant-aware chunk IDs; read-side filter
  status (#11632 pending); parser-execution boundary policy; KB-as-cache vs
  MC-as-store recovery model; auth-flow invariant.
- MigrationPath.md — zero-config upgrade for existing single-repo deployments;
  what stays the same vs what an operator opts into; config-template clone-sync;
  no-breaking-changes inventory; deprecation timeline.

Discipline — strictly invariant-focused per the #11627 audit constraint: every
section describes the SHIPPED substrate (#11647/#11659/#11661/#11662 contracts);
Phase-2-pending surfaces (ingestSourceFiles endpoint, tenant-config persistence
#11637, parser-execution runtime, auth handshake) are marked explicit
`[Phase 2/3 — pending]` placeholders — no endpoint-name invention.

Phase 3B (contract-exact Configuration/CustomSources/CustomParsers docs) +
Phase 3C (runnable examples/hooks) stay blocked by #11637 + Phase 2 #11626 —
PR uses `Related: #11627`, ticket stays open for those slices.

Substrate-path note: `learn/agentos/**` guide docs are conditionally-loaded
World-Atlas reference content (KB-sync-ingested on demand), NOT always-loaded
turn substrate — zero always-loaded-byte accretion.

(#11627)

- 2026-05-21T00:41:25Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `17fa1c3` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomParsers cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — second of the four
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide documents.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomParsers.md — how a cloud tenant
turns its own file formats into Knowledge Base chunks:

- The parsed-chunk-v1 ingest contract — required/optional fields, the
  forbidden embedding field, hashInputs semantics, additionalProperties:false.
- Client-side vs server-side parsing as a trust decision — client-side
  (push parsedChunks; the only path for non-JS formats; no code runs on the
  deployment) vs server-side (registered parser class, operator-gated).
- Authoring a server-side parser — registration via aiConfig.customParsers /
  SourceRegistry.registerParser; the parseIngestionFile(file,{tenantContext})
  modern contract vs the legacy parse() contract the built-in parsers use;
  KB_PARSER_NOT_REGISTERED + the raw-text fallback.
- The parser-execution trust boundary, cross-linked to Security.md.

Endpoint-exact — verified against the merged parsed-chunk-v1 schema, the
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks dispatch ladder, SourceRegistry,
and the built-in SourceParser/DocumentationParser precedent. The remaining
Phase 3B docs (CustomSources, Configuration) + worked examples land in
follow-up commits on this branch; #11627 stays open until the PR completes.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"

configuration surface:

- The two configuration layers — deployment-wide aiConfig (config.mjs) vs
  per-tenant KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig graph storage.
- The cloud-relevant aiConfig keys — useDefaultSources/useDefaultParsers,
  customSources/customParsers, sourcePaths; defaultTenantId/defaultRepoSlug/
  defaultVisibility, spoofRejectionMode, mcpSyncMaxChunks; transport/
  mcpHttpPort/publicUrl/auth for SSE cloud deployments — each with defaults.
- Per-tenant config storage (#11637): the KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig node,
  getTenantConfig 3-tier resolution (graph node -> kb-config.yaml -> aiConfig),
  setTenantConfig versioning + RLS gate, the kb-config.yaml bootstrap, the
  tenantConfigVersion chunk stamp.
- Zero-config inheritance — the default-resolved tier.

Endpoint-exact — config keys verified against config.template.mjs; the
#11637 tenant-config surface against the #11637 Contract Ledger + PR #11704
(APPROVED). Remaining Phase 3B work (CustomSources.md, worked examples,
tree.json integration) lands in follow-up commits; #11627 stays open until
the PR completes the guide tree.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T00:55:52Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `59ebc23` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomSources cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the fourth and final
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide document.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomSources.md — authoring a custom
Source class to index a new content territory in the full-corpus build:

- Source vs Parser — when each applies; most cloud tenants need Custom
  Parsers (push path); Custom Sources serve the full-corpus build path.
- The Source contract — extends source/Base.mjs, async extract(writeStream,
  createHashFn): Promise<Number>, the JSONL chunk-record shape ({type, kind,
  name, content, source} + chunk.hash = createHashFn(chunk)), documented per
  the verified AdrSource precedent.
- A worked custom Source example modelled on AdrSource.
- Registration via aiConfig.customSources / SourceRegistry.registerSource;
  the useDefaultSources gate.
- Path conventions (aiConfig.sourcePaths) + identity-tuple semantics — the
  Source emits content; createHashFn + the write-side stamp own identity.

Documents the extract contract from the verified built-in-Source precedent
(AdrSource + DatabaseService.createContentHash), distinct from the
parsed-chunk-v1 push contract. Completes the four-file Phase 3B guide set;
remaining Phase 3B work — the examples/cloud-deployment/ worked example,
learn/tree.json integration, and the Phase 3A forward-pointer refresh —
lands in follow-up commits before the PR opens. #11627 stays open.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T01:05:29Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `d3cd505` - "docs(examples): add Phase 3B cloud-deployment worked examples (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the runnable worked
examples accompanying the learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/ guide tree.

Adds examples/cloud-deployment/:
- minimal-external-workspace/ — an external tenant workspace (neo.mjs as a
  dependency): package.json, a sample proto/example.proto, src/ProtoParser.mjs
  (custom Parser — parseIngestionFile -> parsed-chunk-v1, the push path),
  src/ProtoSource.mjs (custom Source — extract(), the full-corpus path), and a
  README documenting registration + the smoke-test path.
- pre-push-hook.sh — a git pre-push hook streaming a push's changed files into
  the KB via the ai:ingest-tenant bulk facade; deletion signaling marked as the
  documented HookWiring.md extension point.
- README.md — the example-set overview.

The custom Source/Parser are contract-faithful — ProtoParser emits all 11
parsed-chunk-v1 required fields (no embedding); ProtoSource follows the verified
AdrSource extract precedent. Syntax-verified: node --check on both .mjs files,
bash -n on the hook, valid package.json.

Completes the #11627 guide-tree + examples authoring. The remaining Phase 3B
step — learn/tree.json integration + the Phase 3A forward-pointer refresh —
lands in a follow-up commit; #11627 stays open until the PR.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T01:18:54Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #11707
- 2026-05-21T01:37:36Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `2cb8e90` - "docs(examples): correct the minimal-workspace smoke-test parser contract (#11627)

PR #11707 Cycle-1 review (@neo-gpt RA2): the minimal-external-workspace README
smoke-test claimed a raw .proto push would use the server-side ProtoParser, but
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks defaults a file with no parserId
to the raw-text fallback (one whole-file chunk).

Reworks the Smoke test section to be accurate: the no-parserId push exercises
the raw-text fallback (one chunk, no registration needed); a second example
adds parserId: "proto" + the ProtoParser registration for per-protobuf-message
chunking. No prose now claims a chunking behavior the dispatch ladder does not
perform for the shown command.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T01:49:29Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `836298d` - "docs(examples): correct the pre-push hook's raw-text parser comment (#11627)

PR #11707 Cycle-2 review (@neo-gpt RA-A): the pre-push-hook.sh comment said
"the server parses raw files with a registered parser" — carrying the same
parser-selection misconception Cycle-1 RA2 corrected in the workspace README.
The hook emits {sourcePath, content} records with no parserId, so
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks applies the raw-text fallback
(one whole-file chunk); it does not select a registered parser by extension.

Reworks the comment to match the emitted payload: the no-parserId stream uses
the raw-text fallback, and a deployment with a registered parser adds the
matching parserId to chunk per that parser's contract.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T06:11:47Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `06685f3` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B HookWiring cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — continues the #11627
cloud-deployment guide tree past the Phase 3A invariant scaffold (PR #11668).

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/HookWiring.md — the operator-facing guide
for wiring git hooks to the KB ingestion facades, now that Phase 2 has shipped
the endpoint shapes:

- The two facades and the #10572 work-volume gate that forks them — the
  ingest_source_files MCP operation (#11634, gated) vs the ai:ingest-tenant
  bulk CLI (#11635, viaMcp:false ungated).
- Dual transport (stdio local / sse StreamableHTTP cloud) per Server.mjs.
- The ingest_source_files push envelope, the KB_INGEST_VOLUME_EXCEEDED refusal
  payload, the structured ingestion summary.
- Deletion signaling — tombstones / manifest / revision-boundary, the
  precedence order, and the #11637 gating on revision-boundary resolution.
- pre-push and post-commit hook patterns; the structured-error contract.

Endpoint-exact throughout — every shape verified against the merged Phase 2
substrate (ingestTenant.mjs, KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.mjs, openapi.yaml,
the parser/ contract docs). The remaining Phase 3B docs (CustomSources,
CustomParsers, Configuration) + the worked examples land in follow-up commits
on this branch; #11627 stays open until the PR completes the guide tree.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T06:11:48Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `84e9a59` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomParsers cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — second of the four
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide documents.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomParsers.md — how a cloud tenant
turns its own file formats into Knowledge Base chunks:

- The parsed-chunk-v1 ingest contract — required/optional fields, the
  forbidden embedding field, hashInputs semantics, additionalProperties:false.
- Client-side vs server-side parsing as a trust decision — client-side
  (push parsedChunks; the only path for non-JS formats; no code runs on the
  deployment) vs server-side (registered parser class, operator-gated).
- Authoring a server-side parser — registration via aiConfig.customParsers /
  SourceRegistry.registerParser; the parseIngestionFile(file,{tenantContext})
  modern contract vs the legacy parse() contract the built-in parsers use;
  KB_PARSER_NOT_REGISTERED + the raw-text fallback.
- The parser-execution trust boundary, cross-linked to Security.md.

Endpoint-exact — verified against the merged parsed-chunk-v1 schema, the
KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.resolveFileChunks dispatch ladder, SourceRegistry,
and the built-in SourceParser/DocumentationParser precedent. The remaining
Phase 3B docs (CustomSources, Configuration) + worked examples land in
follow-up commits on this branch; #11627 stays open until the PR completes.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T06:11:48Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `0dfc21c` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B Configuration cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — third of the four
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide documents; fulfils the
[Phase 3B - pending] Configuration placeholder in MigrationPath.md.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/Configuration.md — the KB ingestion
configuration surface:

- The two configuration layers — deployment-wide aiConfig (config.mjs) vs
  per-tenant KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig graph storage.
- The cloud-relevant aiConfig keys — useDefaultSources/useDefaultParsers,
  customSources/customParsers, sourcePaths; defaultTenantId/defaultRepoSlug/
  defaultVisibility, spoofRejectionMode, mcpSyncMaxChunks; transport/
  mcpHttpPort/publicUrl/auth for SSE cloud deployments — each with defaults.
- Per-tenant config storage (#11637): the KnowledgeBaseTenantConfig node,
  getTenantConfig 3-tier resolution (graph node -> kb-config.yaml -> aiConfig),
  setTenantConfig versioning + RLS gate, the kb-config.yaml bootstrap, the
  tenantConfigVersion chunk stamp.
- Zero-config inheritance — the default-resolved tier.

Endpoint-exact — config keys verified against config.template.mjs; the
#11637 tenant-config surface against the #11637 Contract Ledger + PR #11704
(APPROVED). Remaining Phase 3B work (CustomSources.md, worked examples,
tree.json integration) lands in follow-up commits; #11627 stays open until
the PR completes the guide tree.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T06:11:48Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `96b3d50` - "docs(agentos): add Phase 3B CustomSources cloud-deployment guide (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the fourth and final
Phase-2-dependent cloud-deployment guide document.

Adds learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/CustomSources.md — authoring a custom
Source class to index a new content territory in the full-corpus build:

- Source vs Parser — when each applies; most cloud tenants need Custom
  Parsers (push path); Custom Sources serve the full-corpus build path.
- The Source contract — extends source/Base.mjs, async extract(writeStream,
  createHashFn): Promise<Number>, the JSONL chunk-record shape ({type, kind,
  name, content, source} + chunk.hash = createHashFn(chunk)), documented per
  the verified AdrSource precedent.
- A worked custom Source example modelled on AdrSource.
- Registration via aiConfig.customSources / SourceRegistry.registerSource;
  the useDefaultSources gate.
- Path conventions (aiConfig.sourcePaths) + identity-tuple semantics — the
  Source emits content; createHashFn + the write-side stamp own identity.

Documents the extract contract from the verified built-in-Source precedent
(AdrSource + DatabaseService.createContentHash), distinct from the
parsed-chunk-v1 push contract. Completes the four-file Phase 3B guide set;
remaining Phase 3B work — the examples/cloud-deployment/ worked example,
learn/tree.json integration, and the Phase 3A forward-pointer refresh —
lands in follow-up commits before the PR opens. #11627 stays open.

- 2026-05-21T06:11:49Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `73f2ad2` - "docs(examples): add Phase 3B cloud-deployment worked examples (#11627)

Phase 3B of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion) — the runnable worked
examples accompanying the learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/ guide tree.

Adds examples/cloud-deployment/:
- minimal-external-workspace/ — an external tenant workspace (neo.mjs as a
  dependency): package.json, a sample proto/example.proto, src/ProtoParser.mjs
  (custom Parser — parseIngestionFile -> parsed-chunk-v1, the push path),
  src/ProtoSource.mjs (custom Source — extract(), the full-corpus path), and a
  README documenting registration + the smoke-test path.

  the KB via the ai:ingest-tenant bulk facade; deletion signaling marked as the
  documented HookWiring.md extension point.
- README.md — the example-set overview.

The custom Source/Parser are contract-faithful — ProtoParser emits all 11
parsed-chunk-v1 required fields (no embedding); ProtoSource follows the verified
AdrSource extract precedent. Syntax-verified: node --check on both .mjs files,
bash -n on the hook, valid package.json.
